_meta.description: Defines all window.postMessage() messages exchanged between the configurator iframe and the parent fenstermaxx24.com page

parent_page.role: Hosts the configurator iframe, manages site navigation, shopping cart count, and page redirects

iframe_page.role: Runs the full configurator UI, handles all configuration logic, communicates with backend API
iframe_page.domain_separation: Separate subdomain (confde.) — requires postMessage for cross-origin communication

messages_iframe_to_parent.trigger: User clicks 'In den Warenkorb legen' button AND the /ajax/addWarenkorb/ API call succeeds
messages_iframe_to_parent.purpose: Notify parent page that an item has been successfully added to the shopping cart
expected_parent_action: Update cart item count in navigation header

messages_iframe_to_parent.trigger: After successful cart addition, when user clicks 'Weiter konfigurieren' or similar continue button
messages_iframe_to_parent.purpose: Signal to parent that user wants to continue configuring (stay on page or navigate to next step)
expected_parent_action: Optionally scroll to top or reset configurator for next item

messages_iframe_to_parent.trigger: User clicks 'Neu konfigurieren' or restart button
messages_iframe_to_parent.purpose: Signal to parent that user wants to start a fresh configuration from scratch
expected_parent_action: Reload the iframe with default configuration
Reset any parent-side state related to the current configuration

messages_parent_to_iframe.note: The parent page does not appear to send postMessage commands to the iframe. All configuration state is managed internally within the iframe. The parent only listens for messages from the iframe.

parent_listener_implementation.description: The parent page must implement a message event listener to handle iframe communications
parent_listener_implementation.security_note: Always verify event.origin before processing messages to prevent cross-site scripting attacks

cart_integration_flow.description: Complete flow from configurator to shopping cart

steps.action: Iframe sends POST to /ajax/addWarenkorb/ with full obj_konfig
Backend validates configuration, calculates final price, stores in session/database
Parent updates cart count and shows confirmation UI
User can choose 'do_continue' (keep configuring) or navigate to cart

session_linking.purpose: Links the configurator's PHP session (on confde. subdomain) to the main shop session (on www. domain)
session_linking.timing: Called once during iframe initial load
session_linking.importance: Critical for cart functionality — without this, items added in the iframe won't appear in the parent site's shopping cart

for_your_engine: Your backend must handle the addWarenkorb endpoint and store the full configuration + calculated prices
Your frontend must implement the postMessage listener to update the parent page's cart
If you use a single-page app (no iframe), postMessage is not needed — use direct function calls instead
The session linking (setExternTOSession) is only needed if you use a separate domain for the configurator
for_your_crm: When addWarenkorb is called, also push the configuration data to your CRM
Store the full obj_konfig JSON alongside the calculated prices for order tracking
Use the price breakdown (grundpreis, surcharges, discount) for invoice generation
